title: SSVEP Wang 2016 dataset
description: A steady-state visual evoked potential (SSVEP) brain-computer interface dataset comprising EEG recordings from 34 healthy subjects performing a 40-target speller task using joint frequency and phase modulation. The dataset includes 64-channel EEG data sampled at 250 Hz with stimuli spanning frequencies from 8 to 15.8 Hz at 0.2 Hz resolution, designed to support BCI research and algorithm benchmarking.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Synamps2 system (Neuroscan) with 64 electrodes referenced to Cz, sampled at 1000 Hz and downsampled to 250 Hz. Subjects performed a cue-guided target selection task on a 5×8 stimulus matrix displayed on a 23.6-inch LCD monitor at 60 Hz. Each trial consisted of a 0.5 s cue followed by 5 s of visual stimulation at frequencies ranging from 8 to 15.8 Hz with 0.2 Hz resolution. Online bandpass filtering (0.15–200 Hz) and 50 Hz notch filtering were applied during acquisition.
